question_html:
Make meaningful sentences with the following words:

Proactive, Diverse, Initiate, Prudent, Ubiquitous
solution_html:
Proactive – Factories and industries have to be more proactive about environmental management.

Diverse – Bangladesh has a diverse culture.

Initiate – The school initiated a tree-plantation campaign.

Prudent – It is prudent to save money for emergencies.

Ubiquitous – Mobile phones have become so ubiquitous that it’s hard to imagine life without them.

Mark Distribution:

For making meaningful sentence with the following words (0.5×5) =2.5 marks.

[N.B: Any sentence that is meaningful and correct will get allocated marks]

question_html:
Fill in the blanks with suitable words from the list below:

(List: calm, harsh, knowledge, inclusive, patient, joyful)

An ideal classroom is one where every learner feels seen, heard, and respected. To achieve this, it must be an (a)_____ space that welcomes students of all backgrounds, abilities, and perspectives. Teachers play a vital role in shaping this atmosphere and so they should remain (b) _____ in the face of challenges and avoid using (c) _____ or discouraging language, even when correcting mistakes. A/an (d) _____ environment reduces anxiety and promotes focus, while mutual sharing of (e) _____ enhances the learning experience for all.
solution_html:
(a) inclusive

(b) patient/calm

(c) harsh

(d) calm/joyful/inclusive

(e) knowledge

Mark Distribution:

For filling the blanks with suitable words; (0.5×5) =2.5 marks.

question_html: Write a well-organised paragraph on ‘The Power of Education’ in the space provided, considering the element of a paragraph, such as topic sentence, supporting detail sentences and conclusion.
solution_html:
‘The Power of Education’

Education is one of the most powerful tools for personal growth and societal progress. It enlightens the mind, develops critical thinking, and opens doors to new opportunities. An educated individual is more likely to make informed decisions, contribute to the community, and promote peace and tolerance. Education also empowers people to break the cycle of poverty, challenge injustice, and improve their standard of living. In a broader sense, it builds a knowledgeable, skilled, and responsible citizenry that drives national development. Therefore, the power of education lies not only in gaining knowledge but also in transforming lives and shaping a better future for all.

Mark Distribution:

For writing Title, The Power of Education; 0.5 mark.

For writing, topic sentence such as, it enlightens the mind, develops critical thinking, and opens doors to new opportunities; 1.5 marks.

For writing supporting details such as, an educated individual is more likely to make informed decisions, contribute to the community, and promote peace and tolerance; 1.5 marks.

For writing conclusion such as, Therefore, the power of education lies not only in gaining knowledge but also in transforming lives and shaping a better future for all; 1.5 marks.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the obtained marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]
